---
title = "Project Structure"
---

*Calepin* supports two project types: single documents and collections.

# Document: notebook, article, letter, single webpage, etc.

A single `.qmd` file with no configuration needed:

```
paper.qmd
paper_calepin/           # sidecar directory (created automatically)
  config.toml            # per-document configuration (optional)
  cache/                 # code chunk cache
  files/                 # generated figures
```

# Collection: website, book, documentation, blog, etc.

A `_calepin/config.toml` file at the root defines the project:

```
my-project/
  _calepin/
    config.toml          # project configuration
    partials/            # template overrides
      website/
        page.html
        navbar.html
      html/
        figure.html
  assets/
    style.css
    logo.svg
  index.qmd
  about.qmd
  chapters/
    intro.qmd
    methods.qmd
  output/                # generated by calepin
```

# Key directories

- `_calepin/config.toml`: project configuration (targets, sidebar, navbar, etc.).
- `_calepin/partials/`: template overrides. Drop a file here to replace any built-in template.
- `{stem}_calepin/`: sidecar directory for each document, created automatically to hold cached code output and generated figures.
- `output/`: default output directory for collections (configurable in `config.toml`).

# Scaffolding

```bash
calepin new notebook my-paper.qmd
calepin new website my-site
calepin new book my-book
```
